evidence_text: OpenAI Codex CLI is described as having an internal SWE-task benchmark, but the source says no benchmark numbers were published.
source_excerpt: "Internal SWE-task benchmark mentioned but numbers not published"
note: The source names a benchmark but does not provide the underlying result values.
caveat: The source names a benchmark but does not provide the underlying result values.

evidence_text: OpenAI Codex CLI is described as a local terminal CLI that reads, edits, and runs code locally with user-approved shell commands.
source_excerpt: "Local terminal CLI; open-source on GitHub" and "Reads/edits/runs code locally with user-approved shell commands"
note: This is a packaging and operating-mode claim, not a reported performance result.
caveat: This is a packaging and operating-mode claim, not a reported performance result.

evidence_text: Claude Code is reported with SWE-bench Verified at 72.5% for Opus 4 and Terminal-bench at 43.2%.
note: These figures are benchmark-specific and do not by themselves establish performance on other coding tasks.
caveat: These figures are benchmark-specific and do not by themselves establish performance on other coding tasks.

evidence_text: Claude Code is also reported with Sonnet 3.5 at 49% SWE-bench Verified and 93.7% HumanEval.
note: The source reports these as separate benchmark scores for the Sonnet 3.5 model line.
caveat: The source reports these as separate benchmark scores for the Sonnet 3.5 model line.

evidence_text: Devin is reported at 13.86% end-to-end on a 25% SWE-bench Verified subset, with a prior state of the art listed as 1.96%.
source_excerpt: "SWE-bench Verified 13.86 % end-to-end on 25 % subset – prior SOTA 1.96 %"
note: The reported Devin figure is explicitly limited to a 25% subset rather than the full benchmark.
caveat: The reported Devin figure is explicitly limited to a 25% subset rather than the full benchmark.

evidence_text: Devin is described as planning and executing thousands of shell, editor, and browser steps and producing pull requests.
source_excerpt: "Plans & executes thousands of shell/editor/browser steps; produces PRs, learns new tools autonomously"
note: This is a capability description from the comparison table rather than a quantified benchmark.
caveat: This is a capability description from the comparison table rather than a quantified benchmark.

evidence_text: Replit Agent + Code Repair 7B is described as outperforming GPT-4-Turbo and Claude Opus on Replit-repair and DebugBench, but the source says exact percentages were not published.
source_excerpt: "Outperforms GPT-4-Turbo, Claude Opus on Replit-repair & DebugBench; exact % not published, charts show top rank"
note: The source presents a relative superiority claim while also stating that exact numeric results were not published.
caveat: The source presents a relative superiority claim while also stating that exact numeric results were not published.

evidence_text: GitHub Copilot is reported with a Microsoft WorkLab study saying users were 29% faster, 70% felt more productive, and 85% reached a first draft faster.
source_excerpt: "Microsoft WorkLab study: users 29 % faster, 70 % feel more productive, 85 % reach first draft faster"
note: These are productivity-study results rather than classical coding benchmark scores.
caveat: These are productivity-study results rather than classical coding benchmark scores.

evidence_text: Supermaven is reported with 250 ms latency versus Copilot at 783 ms and a 300k-token context window.
source_excerpt: "Latency: 250 ms vs Copilot 783 ms; 300 k-token context window"
note: The source pairs a latency comparison with a stated context-window size.
caveat: The source pairs a latency comparison with a stated context-window size.

evidence_text: Windsurf Cascade is reported with telemetry claiming 90% of user code is auto-generated and 57 million lines are produced per day.
source_excerpt: "Telemetry: 90 % of user code auto-generated; 57 M lines per day"
note: These are usage telemetry figures, not benchmark results from a standardized coding test.
caveat: These are usage telemetry figures, not benchmark results from a standardized coding test.

evidence_text: Vercel v0 is reported with internal UI-task error-free generation scores of 93.87% for v0-1.5-md and 89.80% for v0-1.5-lg, compared with Claude 4 Opus at 78.43%.
source_excerpt: "Internal UI-task eval: error-free generation 93.87 % (v0-1.5-md), 89.80 % (lg) – beats Claude 4 Opus 78.43 %"
note: The source specifies that these are internal UI-task evaluation numbers rather than a general coding benchmark.
caveat: The source specifies that these are internal UI-task evaluation numbers rather than a general coding benchmark.

evidence_text: The source says coding-agent benchmarks measure different task types and should be matched to the intended workload before declaring a winner.
source_excerpt: "Internal SWE-task benchmark mentioned but numbers not published"
note: The source names a benchmark but does not provide the underlying result values.
caveat: The source names a benchmark but does not provide the underlying result values.
